self_review.context: This is a pre-implementation review of the task definition and current codebase. The goal is to assess completeness, identify overlaps with existing implementation, and determine what needs to be implemented or improved.

findings.finding: Autosave is partially implemented but lacks debounce control and proper state handling. Potential issues include race conditions, concurrent requests, and unclear UI states.

Version creation exists but needs optimization: - Possible duplicate snapshots when content is unchanged - No strict enforcement of version limits - Restore flow may create unnecessary additional versions
Notification retrieval exists but lacks explicit limiting. Risk of returning excessive data and impacting performance.
View counting logic is present but does not include abuse prevention. Needs deduplication (session/user-based) and rate limiting considerations.
Comment UI exists but lacks: - Delete confirmation - Clear deleted-state representation

gaps.gap: Missing debounce + race-condition handling in autosave
No content hashing to prevent duplicate versions
No enforced limit on number of stored versions
No deduplication mechanism for view counting

risks.risk: Autosave may overwrite newer data due to concurrent requests
Version table may grow indefinitely without pruning
Notification endpoint may return excessive payload
View count can be inflated via repeated refresh or abuse
UI inconsistency when deleting comments without confirmation

self_review.decision: Proceed with implementation. Focus on incremental improvements while preserving existing behavior.

next_actions.action: Implement autosave debounce with race-condition protection
Optimize versioning using content hashing and enforce limit (max 20)
Add deduplication and basic rate limiting to view counting
Enhance comment UI with delete confirmation and deleted state
